data.title: Inhibit Progression of Coronary Artery Calcification With Vitamin K in HemoDialysis Patients: The iPACK-HD Study
data.brief_summary: The purpose of this study is to see if vitamin K supplementation three times per week reduces the progression of coronary artery calcification over 12 months in dialysis patients compared to placebo.
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Able to provide signed informed consent
* ≥18 years of age
* Expected to survive one year
* Have end-stage kidney disease and require hemodialysis
* Have a baseline coronary artery calcification score ≥30 Agatston units (AUs)

data.sponsor: Clinical Evaluation Research Unit at Kingston General Hospital

data.title: Safety and Efficacy of Intensive Versus Guideline Antiplatelet Therapy in High Risk Patients With Recent Ischaemic Stroke or Transient Ischaemic Attack: a Randomised Controlled Trial
data.brief_summary: The risk of recurrence is greatest immediately after stroke or Transient Ischaemic Attack (TIA). Existing prevention strategies (antithrombotic, lipid/blood pressure lowering, endarterectomy) reduce, not abolish, further events. Dual antiplatelet therapy - aspirin \& clopidogrel (AC) for IHD, aspirin \& dipyridamole (AD) for stroke, is superior to aspirin monotherapy. The investigators hypothesise that triple antiplatelet therapy (ACD) will be superior to AD in patients at high-risk of recurrenc
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

Adults at high risk of recurrent ischaemic stroke:

1. Age ≥ 50 years
2. Within 48 hours of ictus (24-48 hours if thrombolysed)
3. TIA with limb weakness and/or dysphasia lasting between 10 minutes and \< 24 hours with no residual symptoms and presenting with any of the followin
endpoints: The primary outcome is ordinal stroke severity at 90 days
